title: Move Semantics
description: Master Rvalues (&&), std::move, Move Constructors, and Move Assignment Operators to bypass expensive copies.
learningObjectives: Distinguish Lvalues from Rvalues in C++ code structures
Implement custom Move Constructors and Move Assignment Operators
Use std::move to transfer resource pointers without memory allocation

sections.title: Transferring Ownership via Rvalues
sections.content: Before C++11, passing large objects (like arrays or vectors) meant copying their entire contents or dealing with raw pointers. Move semantics introduces Rvalue references (`T&&`), allowing the compiler to 'steal' resources from temporary objects (rvalues) instead of copying them. `std::move` casts an object to an rvalue reference, allowing its state to be pilfered.

sections.title: Implementing a Move Constructor
